response: {"purpose":"Figure 2c displays a histogram of RBFOX2 eCLIP clusters binned by their fold-enrichment relative to the paired Size-Matched Input (SMInput), with clusters overlapping introns flanking RBFOX2-dependent cassette exons highlighted in green. This panel aims to demonstrate that SMInput normalization can distinguish biologically meaningful binding sites—specifically those associated with RBFOX2-regulated splicing events—from background signal across the full distribution of clusters. The enrichment of functionally relevant clusters (cassette exon–flanking introns) at higher fold-enrichment values provides evidence that SMInput normalization improves signal-to-noise in eCLIP data. This matters because it validates the normalization strategy as a principled way to prioritize true RBP binding sites with demonstrated regulatory function.","title":"RBFOX2 eCLIP Cluster Enrichment Over SMInput at Functionally Relevant Sites"}
